rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/14/22. (No earlier availability.) rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AIKO ONODERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/14/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1. Decedent died November 21, 2012. The account must begin on Decedent's date of death November 21, 2012 not November 22, 2012. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. [LRSF 14.34] 2. Please explain the legal basis for how the court can approve $34,253.16 in unsupported reimbursements to Hiroki R. Onodera. It appears Hiroki should be surcharged or his distribution should be reduced by $34,253.16. 3. Please explain the 10 month gap in water bill payments. Between 1/3/2013 and 11/6/2013. 4. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] A file stamped single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/7/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NELLIE GREEN JOHNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for counsel to explain the delay in addressing the following outstanding issues: 1) The estate cash in excess of $1,000,000 has been held at a single institution since September 2021. No proof of fully insuring the estate cash in observance of the FDIC limits has been filed. 2) The decedent died before 06/08/1982 when California inheritance tax was still in effect. No proof of full satisfaction of the tax liability has been filed. 3) No personal representative has been appointed in Mississippi or California for the estate of Ernestine Smith, a postdeceased beneficiary entitled to receive 50% of distribution from this estate. The petition requests direct distribution of Ernestine Smith's share to 29 persons claiming to be her issue and heirs without appointment of the personal representative. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TAMIKO MOORE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom use the Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 and Password: 514879 1) Court to determine how to proceed. No filed response to Examiner notes. 2) Examiner Notes: a. 15 days' required notice must be re-done and include the language required by Local Rule 14.1 D. b. Administrator hasn't filed an acceptance or rejection of all creditor claims. [Probate Code 9250 et seq.] c. Please explain the legal basis/legal authority for the court ordering termination of the estate when there are assets in the estate and creditor claims. It appears this petition should be ordered off calendar and a petition for approval of status report should have been filed instead. d. Please file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the court instructing Chase Bank. e. Please explain all efforts to collect estate assets from Chase from May 2022 to today.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAKO U ROVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/21/22. The amended petition was file stamped on 9/12/22 but not actually e-filed until 9/19/22, when it was first available for viewing. Mailing notice before the filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice is defective. Furthermore, the notice does not indicate that an AMENDED petition has been filed. The service list does not indicate that all heirs received notice. There is also a minor heir-at-law. Notice to a minor must be given directly to the minor and also to the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] That person must be clearly identified in a verified supplement to item 8 of the petition. It appears that the proof of publication was just filed on 10/7/22. If the published notice did not clearly indicate that hearing is of the AMENDED petition, notice must be published again. The caption of the amended petition does not indicate that full IAEA authority is being sought. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/10/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAKO U ROVA

If issues are not addressed in time to deliver the proposed order and courtesy copies of the amended petition and all related papers to the assigned examiner by 11/10/22, an appearance will likely be required on 11/21/22 for the Court to determine if the amended petition should be taken off calendar. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS RUTHERFORD JACKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/14/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. (LRSF 14.16 F.) 3. Petitioner must file and give notice on the mandatory judicial council form DE-121 that complies with Local Rule 14.1 D. 6) 4. Page 4 of the petition under #8 does not state the age of the persons listed and the Decedent's parents and their date of death are not listed. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/31/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
